flashcards.front: What is the general form of recurrence relations solved by the Master Theorem?
flashcards.back: T(n) = aT(n/b) + f(n) where a ≥ 1, b > 1 are constants and f(n) is asymptotically positive.

flashcards.front: What are the three cases of the Master Theorem?
flashcards.back: 1. f(n) = O(n^(log_b a-ε)), 2. f(n) = Θ(n^(log_b a)), 3. f(n) = Ω(n^(log_b a+ε)) with regularity condition.

flashcards.front: Which case of Master Theorem applies to T(n) = 2T(n/2) + n?
flashcards.back: Case 2: Θ(n log n) as f(n) = n and n^(log_2 2) = n.

flashcards.front: What is the regularity condition in Master Theorem's Case 3?
flashcards.back: a⋅f(n/b) ≤ c⋅f(n) for some c < 1 and sufficiently large n.

flashcards.front: What time complexity does Master Theorem give for T(n) = 3T(n/9) + √n?
Can Master Theorem be applied to T(n) = T(n-1) + 1? Why?
flashcards.back: No. Master Theorem requires division form (n/b), not subtraction.

flashcards.front: What is the closed-form solution for T(n) = aT(n/b) + n^c when a = b^c?
flashcards.back: Θ(n^c log n) via Master Theorem Case 2.

flashcards.front: Which sorting algorithm's recurrence is T(n) = 2T(n/2) + O(n)?
flashcards.back: Merge Sort (Case 2 gives Θ(n log n)).

flashcards.front: What is the critical exponent in Master Theorem analysis?
flashcards.back: log_b a - the exponent in n^(log_b a) that determines case selection.

flashcards.front: How does Strassen's algorithm recurrence differ from standard matrix multiplication?
flashcards.back: T(n) = 7T(n/2) + Θ(n²) vs 8T(n/2) + Θ(n²), giving Θ(n^log2 7) vs Θ(n³).

flashcards.front: What is Akra-Bazzi method used for?
flashcards.back: Solving more complex recurrences beyond Master Theorem's scope.
When f(n) is not polynomially smaller/larger, or recurrence isn't in n/b form.
